q: ___ is a set of rules for determining how genetic information in the form of a nucleotide sequence is converted to an amino acid sequence of a protein.
The genetic code consists of a combination of three nucleotides, called a ___.
The minimum combination of the four nucleotides was a ___ code, which could produce $4 \times 4 \times 4$, or 64 possible combinations.
The start codon AUG also codes for the amino acid ___.
UAA, UAG, and UGA are ___ codons.
The genetic code is ___, meaning that more than one codon can code for the same amino acid.
There are only ___ codons that do not code for any amino acid.
Codons that do not code for amino acids serve as ___ signals to end protein synthesis.
The genetic code is ___, meaning that it reads as a series of three-letter codons without spaces, punctuation, or overlap.
The genetic code is nearly ___, meaning almost all organisms build proteins with the same code.
